title: Sexual Harassment

jokes.text: 50 signs your priest might be Michael Jackson
jokes.setup: Michael sends a forward email that sounds religious
jokes.punchline: It's actually about Michael Jackson
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but relies on dated pop culture reference. Shows Michael's juvenile humor.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Guys, I just got this amazing forward email. It's so inspiring, so spiritual. I had to share it with all of you.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: What's it about?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's about this incredible man who changed the world, who brought people together, who had an almost divine gift.

dialogue.speaker: Ryan
dialogue.line: That sounds... religious.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know, right? It's about Michael Jackson.

jokes.text: I am king of forwards
jokes.setup: Michael takes pride in something
jokes.punchline: It's just forwarding emails, not creating content
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael Scott delusion - taking credit for zero creativity. Strong character integration.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm a content creator. I forward emails to the whole office all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: That's just forwarding emails, not creating content.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, I'm curating them.

jokes.text: We're like Friends. I am Chandler. And Joey. And, uh, Pam is Rachel. And Dwight is Kramer.
jokes.setup: Michael compares the office to Friends
jokes.punchline: He assigns himself two characters and puts Kramer (wrong show) as Dwight
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael confusion - wrong show reference and assigning himself multiple characters shows his ego perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay, so I've been thinking about this a lot. Our office is basically like Friends, right? I'm obviously Ross and Chandler combined.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Jim is Joey, Pam is Rachel, and Dwight... Dwight is Kramer.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I'm not Kramer. Kramer's from a different show.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Exactly. You're like the Kramer of our office.

jokes.text: So the monkey does the sex thing right... here! That's funny! That's funny. Not offensive. Because it's nature, educational.
jokes.setup: Michael justifies inappropriate content
jokes.punchline: His logic is completely flawed - it being 'nature' doesn't make it appropriate
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Michael's inability to understand appropriate workplace behavior.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's just nature. Animals do it all the time.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So if it's natural, it can't be inappropriate.

jokes.text: What has two thumbs and likes to bone your mom? This guy!
jokes.setup: Classic 'two thumbs' joke format
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual reference to Ryan's mom
jokes.explanation: Generic crude joke that relies on shock value rather than wit. Shows Michael's inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: Ryan's deadpan reaction to Michael's crude joke
jokes.setup: Michael makes inappropriate joke
jokes.punchline: Ryan's uncomfortable silence/reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic mockumentary beat - the reaction is funnier than the original joke.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: What's up, Halpert? Still queer?
jokes.setup: Packer greets Jim
jokes.punchline: Uses outdated homophobic greeting casually
jokes.explanation: Establishes Packer as offensive character. More character development than actual humor.

dialogue.speaker: Todd Packer
dialogue.line: Hey Jim, what's up, gay?

jokes.text: Todd Packer and I are total BFF. Best friends forever.
jokes.setup: Michael describes friendship with Packer
jokes.punchline: Using teen slang 'BFF' then explaining it shows he's trying too hard to be cool
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's desperate attempt to seem young and cool by over-explaining slang.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Packer and I are BFFs.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Best friends forever.

jokes.text: Packer told them that we were brothers. And so, you know, one thing led to another. And we brought 'em back to the motel. And then Packer did both of them.
jokes.setup: Michael tells story about picking up twins with Packer
jokes.punchline: The 'brother' lie was so Packer could sleep with both while Michael got nothing
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup and payoff showing Michael's delusion about his 'friendship' with Packer. Well-structured story.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So Packer and I, we're at this bar, right? And we see these two identical twins. And Packer goes, 'We should tell them we're brothers.' And I'm like, 'Genius!' So we go up to them and we're like, 'Hey, we're brothers.' And they totally believed us. They were all over us. But then... Packer takes one to his car, and I'm standing there with the other one, and she's like, 'So where's our room?' And I realized... Packer had set the whole thing up so he could sleep with both of them while I got nothing.

jokes.text: It was awesome.
jokes.setup: Michael just described getting nothing from the twin encounter
jokes.punchline: He still thinks it was awesome despite being completely left out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - finding positivity in his own humiliation. Great character consistency.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But it was awesome.

jokes.text: We're talking 'blonde incompetent,' right? Like, uh, ten words a minute. Talking.
jokes.setup: Michael discussing the CFO's secretary
jokes.punchline: His sexist stereotype gets confused - ten words a minute for talking, not typing
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's casual sexism and confusion about his own stereotypes.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: She types ten words a minute. I mean, talks ten words a minute.

jokes.text: They are women, right? Oh, wow! I didn't say it! I didn't say it! I said it.
jokes.setup: Michael makes sexist comment
jokes.punchline: Tries to deny it then immediately admits it in confusion
jokes.explanation: Michael's internal confusion played out loud - good escalation of his panic.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I didn't say that!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, yes I did.

jokes.text: Hey, what has two thumbs and hates Todd Packer? This guy!
jokes.setup: Uses same format as Michael's earlier crude joke
jokes.punchline: Subverts it with wholesome dislike of Packer
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that subverts Michael's crude version with Jim's cleaner sensibility.

dialogue.speaker: Jim
dialogue.line: Why did Packer cross the road? Because he's a terrible person and I hope he gets hit by a car.

jokes.text: That Todd Packer can do anything. Except pass that breathalyzer.
jokes.setup: Michael praises Packer's abilities
jokes.punchline: Reveals Packer has DUI problems
jokes.explanation: Good structure with clean setup and punchline, though somewhat predictable.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Todd Packer is an amazing salesman. The man is a machine.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I don't know how he does it.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Although, I did just find out he has three DUIs.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Which is actually... kind of impressive.

jokes.text: You a big William Hung fan? Why does everybody ask me that? Who the hell is that?
jokes.setup: Michael asks Kevin about William Hung
jokes.punchline: Kevin is confused why people ask and doesn't know who that is
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about racial assumptions and dated pop culture.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Do you know who William Hung is?

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: No, why does everybody keep asking me that?

jokes.text: As a child did Pam show any traits that would hint towards her future career as a receptionist?
jokes.setup: Michael asks about meeting Pam's mom
jokes.punchline: Treats being a receptionist like it requires special childhood indicators
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist take on career development, shows Michael's cluelessness about normal jobs.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So when do I get to meet your mom?

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Um, I don't know, that's pretty fast.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, I like to move quickly. I'm very busy.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Okay, well maybe after we've been dating for a while.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, Pam, I think your mom will really like me. I'm very charming. Plus, I have a great job. I'm a regional manager. Do you know what that means? It means I'm in charge. I have people working for me. I have a receptionist. Do you know how hard it is to be a receptionist? You need special skills. You need to be able to answer phones, you need to be able to transfer calls... Most people don't have those skills. Most people couldn't do that job. It requires a certain... je ne sais quoi.

jokes.text: I'm going to forward it like it's hot. Yes! Old school.
jokes.setup: Michael wants the monkey video link
jokes.punchline: Parodies 'Drop It Like It's Hot' with 'forward it like it's hot'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun on popular song, shows Michael trying to be hip but dated.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I need that monkey video link. Forward it like it's hot, like it's hot, like it's hot.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: That's not how the song goes.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I know how it goes, Kevin. But this is better. Forward it like it's hot.

jokes.text: Toby is in HR, which technically means he works for corporate. So he's really not a part of our family. Also, he's divorced, so he's really not a part of his family.
jokes.setup: Michael explains why Toby isn't part of their work family
jokes.punchline: Extends logic to say he's not part of his actual family either due to divorce
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of Michael's hatred for Toby. Perfect structure and character voice. Quotable and memorable.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: If you think about it, Toby isn't really part of our work family. And since he's not part of our work family, and our work family is an extension of our actual family, that means he's not part of our actual family either.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: And if he's not part of our actual family, then he's certainly not part of his own actual family. Which explains the divorce.

jokes.text: There is no such thing as an appropriate joke. That's why it's a joke.
jokes.setup: Discussion about inappropriate jokes
jokes.punchline: Michael's circular logic that all jokes are inherently inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's flawed understanding of humor and appropriateness with confident delivery.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Look, I don't think there's anything wrong with a good joke. The problem is when people get offended by them.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: But all jokes are about something that could offend someone, right? So technically, all jokes are inappropriate.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Which means if you tell a joke that doesn't offend anyone, is it even a joke? I don't think so.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So really, I'm doing everyone a favor by telling inappropriate jokes. I'm keeping comedy alive.

jokes.text: But you are going to have a mutiny on your hands. And I just can't wait to see how you handle it.
jokes.setup: Michael 'warns' Toby about the sexual harassment training
jokes.punchline: He's clearly looking forward to undermining Toby rather than helping
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Michael's passive-aggressive nature toward Toby.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Toby, I'm going to be honest with you. I don't think you're qualified to teach this course.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Michael, I've been conducting these trainings for five years.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, that doesn't make you qualified. That makes you persistent. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm going to help you. I'm going to make sure this training goes really well.

jokes.text: A guy goes to a five dollar... lady of the night. And he gets crabs. So the next day, he goes back to complain. And the woman says, 'Hey, it was only five dollars. What did you expect, lobster?'
jokes.setup: Man gets STD from cheap prostitute
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'crabs' as both STD and seafood
jokes.explanation: Generic bar joke with simple pun. Shows Michael's reliance on crude, unoriginal humor.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So this guy goes to a cheap prostitute and gets crabs. His doctor says, 'Well, the good news is you can treat it with this cream.' Guy says, 'What's the bad news?' Doctor says, 'The bad news is you're gonna have an irresistible craving for Old Bay seasoning.'

jokes.text: This is what's at stake.
jokes.setup: Michael just told a crude joke
jokes.punchline: He presents this generic joke as if it's high art worth preserving
jokes.explanation: Michael's delusional seriousness about protecting bad humor is perfectly in character.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm gonna have that one bronzed. That's comedy gold right there. We need to preserve that for future generations.

jokes.text: Where did you get 'em, like, Queers 'R' Us? Boys 'R' Us.
jokes.setup: Warehouse workers mock Michael's tight pants
jokes.punchline: Play on Toys 'R' Us with homophobic reference, then correction
jokes.explanation: Basic wordplay with offensive elements, shows warehouse culture but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: We can see all your business coming around the corner. Good thing you don't have a lot of business to start with.
jokes.setup: Comments about Michael's tight pants showing his anatomy
jokes.punchline: Insults his size to soften the first observation
jokes.explanation: Standard roast format, establishes warehouse workers as crude but not particularly witty.

dialogue.speaker: Warehouse Worker
dialogue.line: Hey, those pants are really tight. We can see everything.

dialogue.speaker: Warehouse Worker
dialogue.line: Yeah, but there's not much to see anyway.

jokes.text: He get it from his mama!
jokes.setup: Compliments Michael's appearance in tight pants
jokes.punchline: References his mother as source of his looks
jokes.explanation: Generic pop culture reference without much wit or originality.

dialogue.speaker: Warehouse worker
dialogue.line: Whoa, those pants are tight! You're looking good!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Thanks! I got my looks from my mother.

dialogue.speaker: Warehouse worker
dialogue.line: Your mother must be one fine woman!

jokes.text: Mmmmilf.
jokes.setup: Pam mentions her mom is coming to visit
jokes.punchline: Kevin immediately sexualizes her mother
jokes.explanation: Perfectly in character for Kevin but relies on crude shock value rather than wit.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Your mom's coming to visit? Is she single?

jokes.text: Let's just act every day like Pam's mom is coming in.
jokes.setup: Pam asks people not to be inappropriate around her mother
jokes.punchline: Michael suggests this impossible standard of constant good behavior
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's inability to understand why this would be difficult - good character comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So what you're saying is, for the next 24 hours, we all have to be on our best behavior. No inappropriate comments, no crude jokes, no—

jokes.text: That is my daughter. She goes to Catholic girl's school. I'm taking it down right now.
jokes.setup: Michael describes Stanley's 'hot' picture as sexual
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's his innocent daughter in school uniform
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that subverts expectations perfectly. Shows Michael's inappropriate assumptions.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wow, Stanley, this is a really hot picture.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: That's my daughter.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, she is a very attractive woman.

dialogue.speaker: Stanley
dialogue.line: She's in high school.

jokes.text: She wasn't that hot. Yes, she was. Dammit, Kevin!
jokes.setup: Michael mentions a former employee was hot
jokes.punchline: Kevin disagrees, Michael insists, then gets mad at Kevin for disagreeing
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's petty need to be right about everything, even attractiveness judgments.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: She was hot.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: I don't think so.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What? Yes, she was. She was very hot.

dialogue.speaker: Kevin
dialogue.line: Not really.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Kevin, she was hot! Why are you being difficult about this?

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Put on a shirt! I told you that you'd be on camera. I'm sorry, she's European.
jokes.setup: Michael mentions his office relationship
jokes.punchline: His 'girlfriend' appears topless on camera, he blames her being European
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag that reveals Michael's desperation and cultural stereotyping.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I have a girlfriend. She's European.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: She doesn't understand American customs about, you know, shirts.

jokes.text: Pam, you will be girl A. And girl B will be... Okay, we'll use the doll.
jokes.setup: Michael wants to act out lesbian scenario
jokes.punchline: Pam refuses and he resorts to using a doll
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate request met with Pam's refusal creates good cringe moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Come on, Pam, just play along. We'll do a little improv scene.

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Michael, no. Absolutely not.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Why not? It'll be funny!

dialogue.speaker: Pam
dialogue.line: Because I'm not doing that.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Fine. Fine! I'll just use this doll.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay doll, you be the lesbian. I'll be the other lesbian.

jokes.text: That's what she said... That's what she said... No, but... okay.
jokes.setup: Normal conversation about watching video and Pam's mom coming
jokes.punchline: Michael forces inappropriate 'that's what she said' into innocent contexts
jokes.explanation: Establishes Michael's signature catchphrase and his inability to use it appropriately.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: So you're watching a video and Pam's mom is coming over?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: Where is the clitoris?
jokes.setup: Dwight has questions for Toby
jokes.punchline: Asks extremely personal anatomical question in professional setting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight moment - direct and inappropriate question delivered matter-of-factly.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Toby, I have a question for you. Is it true that you have a third nipple?

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: What? No. Why would you ask me that?

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: I heard it from Pam. She said Jim told her that you mentioned it at the Christmas party.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: I did not say that.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: So you're denying it? Because that's what people with third nipples always say.

jokes.text: Technically, I am in human resources. And Dwight was asking about human anatomy. I'm just sad the public school system failed him so badly.
jokes.setup: Dwight asks inappropriate anatomical question
jokes.punchline: Toby rationalizes helping while expressing disappointment in education system
jokes.explanation: Excellent Toby response showing his awkward attempts to be helpful while maintaining professionalism.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: Toby, I have a question about human anatomy. Is it anatomically possible for a woman to—

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Dwight, I really don't think this is appropriate for the workplace.

dialogue.speaker: Dwight
dialogue.line: It's a legitimate medical question.

dialogue.speaker: Toby
dialogue.line: Fine. Yes, it's possible. And you know what? This is exactly what's wrong with our education system. Kids aren't learning basic biology anymore, so grown men have to ask HR representatives in the office. It's really quite disappointing.

jokes.text: I banged this girl right here. Yes, this is the one. You banged her? Right here. You are a naughty girl.
jokes.setup: Corporate training video plays
jokes.punchline: Michael inappropriately claims to have slept with actress while Darryl actually did
jokes.explanation: Michael's inappropriate behavior during training video creates cringe comedy.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yeah, I've actually slept with her.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: No you haven't.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Yes I have.

dialogue.speaker: Darryl
dialogue.line: Michael, I'm the one who actually slept with her.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, I slept with her first.

jokes.text: The girl in the video we're watching that corporate gave us. Darryl banged her! And... He's about 90% sure.
jokes.setup: Michael explains the situation in talking head
jokes.punchline: Adds uncertainty percentage that undermines the whole claim
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of the dramatic moment with the uncertainty qualifier.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm 90% sure that's not a real word.

jokes.text: You wouldn't arrest a guy who's just delivering drugs from one guy to another.
jokes.setup: Michael explains he just forwards emails, doesn't create them
jokes.punchline: Compares email forwarding to drug dealing as if they're equivalent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd analogy showing Michael's warped logic and desperation. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I don't create emails. I just forward them. It's not a crime.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: It's like being a drug dealer, except I'm not selling drugs. I'm just passing along what someone else made.

jokes.text: And you can consider this my retirement from comedy.
jokes.setup: Michael announces workplace behavior changes
jokes.punchline: Treats his bad jokes as if they were a professional comedy career
jokes.explanation: Michael's grandiose view of his own humor perfectly captured in this declaration.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Okay everyone, I need to announce some new workplace behavior changes. From now on, I'm going to be treating my jokes as a professional comedy career.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: See, that's gold. I should be getting paid for this.

jokes.text: Well, you always left me satisfied and smiling, so... That's what she said!
jokes.setup: Jan innocently describes Michael's work performance
jokes.punchline: Perfect setup for 'that's what she said' which Michael can't resist
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff. Jan unknowingly delivers ideal setup, Michael can't help himself despite trying to reform. Iconic moment.

dialogue.speaker: Jan
dialogue.line: Your performance has really come up this quarter.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: That's what she said.

jokes.text: You would have done the same. You just didn't think of it first.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to justify his inappropriate joke
jokes.punchline: Claims everyone would have made the same joke, they're just slower
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing Michael's inability to take responsibility for his actions.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Come on, everyone would have made that joke. You're just all slower than me.

jokes.text: So I'm not in trouble? I am so used to being the bad boy. I am so used to fighting corporate that I forget that I am corporate.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes he's not in trouble
jokes.punchline: Sees himself as rebel when he's actually management
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael delusion - seeing himself as anti-establishment when he is the establishment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Wait, I'm not in trouble? I'm management! I'M the man! I make the rules!

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I see myself as a rebel, you know? But I'm actually... I'm the establishment. I'm the guy keeping everyone down.

jokes.text: Mo' money, mo' problems.
jokes.setup: Michael realizes he's upper management
jokes.punchline: Uses Biggie lyric to describe his management position
jokes.explanation: Generic pop culture reference that doesn't really fit the situation but shows Michael's try-hard coolness.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: I'm upper management now. You know what that means? Mo' money, mo' problems.

jokes.text: She goes, 'Oh, my God. That's incredible. Where are we going?' He goes, 'I don't know where you're going, just be out of here by five.' Boom!
jokes.setup: Wife excited about lottery win asks where they're going
jokes.punchline: Husband is kicking her out, not taking her somewhere
jokes.explanation: Classic joke structure but generic content. Shows Michael still telling inappropriate jokes to Pam's mom.

dialogue.speaker: Wife
dialogue.line: Where are we going?

dialogue.speaker: Husband
dialogue.line: We're not going anywhere. I'm kicking you out.

jokes.text: Like a grandmother. I'm the same age as you, Michael. We're the same high school class.
jokes.setup: Michael calls Phyllis like a grandmother
jokes.punchline: Phyllis reveals they're the same age and went to school together
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment that deflates Michael's condescending comment perfectly.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Hey Phyllis, how are you doing today? You feeling okay?

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: I'm fine, Michael. Why are you talking to me like that?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Well, you know, I just want to make sure you're comfortable. Can I get you anything? Some prune juice maybe?

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Michael, we're the same age. We went to school together.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: What? No, that's... we did not.

jokes.text: Well, I have a late birthday and usually September is a cut off point...
jokes.setup: Phyllis says they're the same age
jokes.punchline: Michael tries to rationalize why he might be younger with irrelevant details
jokes.explanation: Shows Michael's desperate attempts to maintain superiority with nonsensical logic.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: We're the same age.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: No, no, no. I was born in 1966... well, technically '67, but my parents like to say '66 because it was a better year for the economy.

jokes.text: Come here. Give me a kiss. Michael, come on, you don't have to worry. I'm not gonna... I'm not worried. Report you to HR. You know what? The only thing I am worried about is getting a boner.
jokes.setup: Michael awkwardly tries to prove he finds Phyllis attractive
jokes.punchline: Takes it way too far by mentioning potential arousal
jokes.explanation: Peak Michael inappropriateness - trying to fix one problem by creating a worse one.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Phyllis, you are a beautiful woman. I mean it. Those eyes, that smile... I find you very attractive.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Oh, Michael, that's... thank you?

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: In fact, if I'm being completely honest, you're giving me feelings right now. Physical feelings. In my pants area.

dialogue.speaker: Phyllis
dialogue.line: Oh my God...

jokes.text: And at some point, the daddy can't take a bath with the kids anymore. I am upper management. And it would be inappropriate for me to take a bath with Pam. As much as I might want to.
jokes.setup: Michael uses family metaphor for workplace relationships
jokes.punchline: Extends bath metaphor inappropriately to employee relationships, especially Pam
jokes.explanation: Brilliant/horrible escalation of family metaphor that reveals Michael's inappropriate thoughts. Classic ending moment.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: You know, the office is like a family. And family bathes together.

dialogue.speaker: Michael
dialogue.line: Pam, get in the tub.

tmdb_overview: Just as corporate tells Toby, one of the workers at the Scranton branch, to give a seminar on sexual harassment issues, Michael receives a visit from his obnoxious sales-rep friend Todd Packer.  The two chauvinistic friends try to prove that the office is not in need of harassment counseling, but only wind up making the situation worse, causing yet another zany day at the office.
insight: Cringe comedy carries a 70-score episode built on Michael's relentless harassment training obliviousness.
